title: Feuilleea negrensis

Further studies are required to fully understand the medicinal properties and potential uses of Feuilleea negrensis.

intro:
Unfortunately, I couldn't find any information about the plant Feuilleea negrensis. It's possible that it's a rare or newly discovered plant species, or it may be a misspelling or variation of a different plant name.

However, I can suggest a possible alternative plant that might be being referred to: Feijoa (Acca sellowiana) is not it but I did find a reference to Feijoa's close relative Fevillea (Fevillea cordifolia) which is actually a different plant and not the same as the plant you mentioned, but I did find that  Fevillea negrensis is a synonym of  Fevillea cordifolia.

If you are actually referring to Fevillea cordifolia here are some possible facts about the plant:

Fevillea cordifolia, also known as the cordifolia, is a plant species in the Cucurbitaceae family. It is native to tropical regions of South America and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Fevillea cordifolia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Fevillea cordifolia is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and may also be used in combination with other herbs to treat various health conditions.
